kill_fact: No kill_fact breaks the BEAR case — the bear thesis SURVIVES the kill-check intact. Every load-bearing bear figure verifies against primary sources: Q1-2026 revenue $503,389 (net loss $12.3M, op cash burn $8.8M), the $400M raise at $21.00/unit (19,047,620 shares + matching $26.25 warrant), and the Lumilens $50M PO / $500M framework with verbatim conditional language ('Fulfilment of the purchase orders and associated revenues is subject to the successful development and ultimate qualification of the modules, as well as the successful scaling of manufacturing capability'; samples late-2026, production 2027). The valuation gap is WORSE than the dossier stated: the stock now trades ~$13.28 vs a ~$8.20 sell-side median (~62% above, vs the dossier's ~48% at $12.15). sop_fair_value (~$5.50) is built on POST-event Q1-2026 financials and the post-$400M-raise share/cash base — NO stale-anchor problem. This is a momentum-priced pre-revenue option (~$2.1B cap on ~$2M annualized revenue) whose load-bearing catalyst is explicitly conditional, third-party-gated and 2027-dated. The bear PASS-as-C / conviction-2 stands.
corrections: DILUTION UNDERSTATED: the dossier cites '~19M warrants' but the Lumilens deal grants an ADDITIONAL warrant for up to 22,921,408 common shares at $8.25 (2,292,140 immediately exercisable, rest vesting on cumulative $500M payments, 9-yr term) — struck BELOW the current price, so it is a live ~13% incremental dilution overhang on top of the separate 19,047,620-share $400M-raise warrant at $26.25. The total potential share count / per-share drag is materially worse than the dossier's '63% YoY + ~19M warrants' framing. This STRENGTHENS the bear (it does not rescue the long). Minor: net loss is $12.3M (the dossier's op-loss '~$17.9M' figure is not the headline GAAP net loss; operating cash burn $8.8M/qtr is the right number to anchor on and is correct).
evidence: POET Technologies & Lumilens release (poet-technologies.com, 2026-05-14): $50M initial PO for EOI-based engines, framework scaling to $500M+ over 5 yrs; VERBATIM conditional clause 'Fulfilment of the purchase orders and associated revenues is subject to the successful development and ultimate qualification of the modules, as well as the successful scaling of manufacturing capability'; engineering samples late-2026, production aligned to hyperscaler deployments 2027; Lumilens warrant up to 22,921,408 shares at $8.25, 9-yr.

POET SEC Form FWP / 6-K (sec.gov EDGAR CIK 0001437424, filed ~2026-05-15): $400,000,020 registered direct offering — 19,047,620 common shares + warrant for 19,047,620 shares at combined US$21.00/unit; warrant exercise US$26.25, 3-yr; use of proceeds incl. 'targeted acquisitions' + light-source business + manufacturing infrastructure; closed 2026-05-18.

POET Q1-2026 results (Globe and Mail / StockTitan / Yahoo Finance, reported ~2026-05-14): NRE & product revenue $503,389 vs $166,760 Q1-25 (+194% YoY, beat ~$250K est); net loss $12.3M / $(0.08) per share; operating cash flow $(8.8)M; ~$429-430M cash on hand.

Analyst consensus (MarketBeat / StockAnalysis, June 2026): median price target ~$8.20 (range $8.00-$8.40), 4 analysts; stock trading ~$13.28 — ~62% ABOVE the sell-side median, i.e. the consensus-delta runs the WRONG way for a long.
